title: Consolidate ISBN fields from two to one
description:
# ISBN Field Consolidation

## Summary
Consolidate ISBN fields in the data model from two separate fields (`isbn` and `isbn13`) to a single canonical ISBN field. Since ISBN-13 is the modern standard and ISBN-10 can be mathematically derived from ISBN-13, we only need to store one field.

## Background
Currently the `Book` interface has two ISBN fields:
- `isbn?: string` (ISBN-10 format, 10 digits)
- `isbn13?: string` (ISBN-13 format, 13 digits)

This creates redundancy and confusion. When you have an ISBN-13, you can derive the ISBN-10 by:
1. Remove the "978" prefix
2. Recalculate the check digit using the ISBN-10 algorithm

## Scope of Changes

### 1. Update Book Interface (`src/types/index.ts`)
- Remove `isbn?: string` field
- Keep `isbn13?: string` field as the canonical ISBN field
- Add computed/getter property for ISBN-10 derived from ISBN-13
- Update all TypeScript types that reference these fields

### 2. Update ScanQueueItem Interface
- Remove `isbn13?: string` field (keep the required `isbn` field as ISBN-13)
- Ensure barcode scanning stores results in canonical ISBN-13 format

### 3. Update ImportBookData Interface
- Remove `isbnSource?: string` field
- Keep single `isbn?: string` field as ISBN-13

### 4. Update Code References
Search for and update all references to:
- `book.isbn` → `book.isbn13`
- `isbn10` → derived from `isbn13`
- `isbn13` → keep as canonical field

### 5. Add Utility Functions
```typescript
// Convert ISBN-13 to ISBN-10
function isbn13ToIsbn10(isbn13: string): string | null

// Validate ISBN format
function isValidIsbn(isbn: string): boolean

// Normalize ISBN (remove hyphens, validate check digit)
function normalizeIsbn(isbn: string): string | null
```

### 6. Database/Storage Migration
- If using local storage (IndexedDB, etc.), migrate existing records
- Update any server-side schemas if applicable

### 7. API Updates
- Update any external API integrations that expect both ISBN formats
- Google Books API returns both, so we need to store only ISBN-13

## Technical Details

### ISBN Derivation Logic
ISBN-13: 978-{1-9 digit group}-{publisher code}-{check digit}
ISBN-10: {1-9 digit group}-{publisher code}-{computed check digit}

Example:
- ISBN-13: 9780470059029
- ISBN-10: 0470059029 (remove 978, compute new check digit)

### Check Digit Calculation
ISBN-10 check digit: (Σ(digit_i × (10-i))) mod 11, mapped to 0-9 or X

## Files to Modify
- `src/types/index.ts` - Book, ScanQueueItem, ImportBookData interfaces
- Any components/services that read/write ISBN fields
- Utility functions in `src/lib/` or similar
- Database/storage layer files

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for ISBN conversion utilities
- Integration tests for book import/scan workflows
- Verify all existing books display correctly after migration
- Test barcode scanning still works with ISBN-13 input

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Single ISBN field (ISBN-13) in Book interface
- [ ] ISBN-10 can be derived on-demand when needed
- [ ] All ISBN-related tests pass
- [ ] No breaking changes to user-facing functionality
- [ ] Documentation updated with ISBN format rationale

## Priority
Medium - reduces data redundancy and simplifies the data model
close_reason: Successfully consolidated ISBN fields from two to one. Removed redundant isbn field, kept isbn13 as canonical field, added utility functions, updated all API mappings, database operations, hooks, and UI components. TypeScript compilation verified clean, unit tests passing.

title: Home screen does not show cover images for locally stored covers
description:
# Bug: Home screen does not show cover images for locally stored covers ## Summary The home screen's "Recently Added" section shows placeholder icons instead of actual cover images for books that have covers stored locally in IndexedDB (via ). ## Root Cause In , the component (lines 228-256) only checks for : ```tsx {book.coverUrl ? ( ) : (
)} ``` It completely ignores `localCoverPath`, which is the IndexedDB cover ID used for imported books and manually uploaded covers. ## How It Should Work The type () supports two cover image sources: - `coverUrl`: Remote/external URL from APIs (Google Books, etc.) - `localCoverPath`: IndexedDB cover ID for imported/uploaded books The component () correctly handles both: 1. Checks for `coverUrl` first and uses it directly 2. Falls back to `localCoverPath`, fetching the blob URL from IndexedDB 3. Provides loading states and error handling ## Comparison with Library Page The page correctly uses the component: ```tsx // Line 249 ``` ```tsx // Line 287 ``` ## Expected Fix Replace the inline cover image rendering in with the component: ```tsx // Current (broken): {book.coverUrl ? ( ) : (
)} // Fixed: ``` ## Files Involved - `src/pages/Home.tsx` - Main file needing fix (BookCard component, lines 228-256) - `src/components/image/BookCover.tsx` - Existing reusable component - `src/types/index.ts` - Book interface definition ## Reproduction Steps 1. Import a ZIP file with cover images (or add a book with a local cover) 2. Navigate to the home page 3. Observe that "Recently Added" books show placeholder icons instead of their cover images 4. Navigate to Library page - covers display correctly there

title: Implement cover image cropping UI component

## Core Features

## Acceptance Criteria

- Aspect ratio maintained
- Mobile touch gestures supported
- Cancel and Apply buttons work correctly

Estimate: 10-16 hours

title: Integrate cropping UI with cover upload flow
description:
Connect the cropping UI component to the existing cover image upload functionality.

## User Flow
1. User selects/uploads cover image
2. System displays cropping UI with the image
3. User crops/pans/zooms to desired area
4. User clicks Apply to confirm
5. System processes cropped image and updates cover

## Integration Points
- Cover Upload Component: Add cropping trigger after file selection
- Image Processing: Handle cropped image conversion and storage
- State Management: Update cover image state after crop
- Error Handling: Graceful fallback if cropping fails

## Dependencies
- Requires: webapp-booky-2kl (Cover Cropping Component)

## UX Requirements
- Clear indication cropping is available after upload
- Smooth transition between upload and crop views
- Immediate feedback after crop applied
- Ability to re-crop image later

## Acceptance Criteria
- Upload flow includes cropping step for large images
- Cropped image replaces original in cover display
- User can cancel cropping and use original
- Cropped images properly optimized and stored
- Existing cover images can be re-cropped

Estimate: 6-12 hours
close_reason: Integrated cropping UI with cover upload flow: Added CoverUpload component, updated BookForm and AddBookPage, added cropping utilities to coverImageUtils.ts. All tests passing.

title: Cropping has the wrong aspect ratio
description:
# Bug: Image Cropping Produces Incorrect Aspect Ratio

## Summary
The image cropping functionality produces images with incorrect aspect ratios, causing visual distortion in cover images.

## Affected Components
- `src/components/image/ImageCropper.tsx` - Main cropper component
- `src/components/image/CoverUpload.tsx` - Upload component using the cropper
- `src/lib/coverImageUtils.ts` - Utility functions

## Root Cause Analysis

### Issue Location: `getCroppedImg` function in `ImageCropper.tsx` (lines 109-163)

The problem occurs in how the cropped image is generated:

1. **Rotation calculation creates larger canvas**: Lines 127-131 calculate the canvas dimensions needed to accommodate the rotated image:
   ```typescript
   const width = image.width * cos + image.height * sin;
   const height = image.width * sin + image.height * cos;
   canvas.width = width;
   canvas.height = height;
   ```

2. **Image is drawn rotated on the larger canvas**: Lines 133-139 translate and rotate the context, then draw the image.

3. **getImageData uses cropPixels from wrong coordinate space**: Lines 142-147 extract image data using `croppedAreaPixels`, which are coordinates calculated by `react-easy-crop` based on the original (unrotated) image dimensions.

4. **Canvas is then resized to cropPixels dimensions**: Lines 149-150 overwrite the canvas size:
   ```typescript
   canvas.width = cropPixels.width;
   canvas.height = cropPixels.height;
   ```

### The Problem

When rotation is non-zero:
- The `croppedAreaPixels` coordinates are relative to the original image
- The rotated canvas has different dimensions than the original
- Using `cropPixels` directly on the rotated canvas produces incorrect results

The aspect ratio gets distorted because the crop coordinates don't account for the transformation matrix applied during rotation.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Upload an image with a non-16:9 aspect ratio
2. When prompted, enter cropping mode
3. Apply a rotation (e.g., 90 degrees)
4. Observe the resulting cropped image has incorrect proportions

## Expected Behavior
The cropped image should maintain the selected aspect ratio (16:9 for covers) regardless of rotation.

## Suggested Fix

The `getCroppedImg` function needs to transform the crop coordinates from the original image space to the rotated canvas space before extracting image data.

This requires:
1. Calculating where `cropPixels.x` and `cropPixels.y` would be on the rotated canvas
2. Adjusting for the translation applied during rotation
3. Ensuring the extracted region maintains the correct aspect ratio

Alternatively, use a simpler approach:
- Apply rotation first to the source image
- Calculate crop on the unrotated image
- Extract the crop region directly

## Impact
- Users see distorted cover images
- Breaks visual consistency of book covers
- Affects any image that requires rotation during cropping
close_reason: Closed

title: Add category management to BookDetail view
description:
# Enable category management in BookDetail view

## Problem Statement

Currently, users cannot manage book categories in the detail view. Categories exist in the book data model (`book.categories?: string[]`) and can be set during book creation/editing via `BookForm.tsx`, but:

1. The BookDetail page does not display categories at all
2. There is no way to add, edit, or remove categories directly from the detail view
3. Users must navigate to the edit page to modify categories

## User Requirements

1. **Display categories in detail view**: Show existing categories as badges/chips in the BookDetail page
2. **Clickable empty state**: When no categories are set, display a clickable element that opens a category selector
3. **Inline category editing**: Provide a category selector (similar to CollectionSelector) to search and add categories
4. **Edit view integration**: Ensure the edit view (BookForm.tsx) properly displays and saves categories (already implemented)

## Technical Requirements

### BookDetail.tsx Changes:
- Add state for showing/hiding category selector: `const [showCategorySelector, setShowCategorySelector] = useState(false);`
- Add categories section to the metadata card or as a separate section
- Display categories as badges when they exist
- Show clickable "Add category" button when no categories exist
- Implement CategorySelector component with:
  - Search functionality for existing categories
  - Ability to add new categories
  - Remove existing categories

### CategorySelector Component (new):
Create a reusable component similar to CollectionSelector:
- Search input for filtering categories
- List of selected categories with remove buttons
- "Add new category" option with input
- Save/cancel actions

### Data Flow:
- Load categories from book data on page load
- Update book.categories array when categories change
- Persist changes via `bookOperations.update()`

### Existing Implementation Reference:
- `BookForm.tsx` lines 179-187: Categories input field
- `src/types/index.ts` line 47: `categories?: string[]`
- CollectionSelector pattern: `src/components/forms/CollectionManager.tsx`

## Implementation Steps

1. Create CategorySelector component (or enhance existing tag-related components)
2. Add category display section to BookDetail.tsx
3. Add category selector with show/hide functionality
4. Implement category add/remove functionality
5. Test category management in both detail view and edit view

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Categories are displayed in BookDetail page as badges
- [ ] Empty category state shows clickable "Add category" button
- [ ] Clicking opens category selector with search functionality
- [ ] Categories can be added, searched, and removed
- [ ] Changes persist to database
- [ ] Categories still work in BookForm edit view
- [ ] Mobile-responsive design
- [ ] Accessible (keyboard navigation, ARIA labels)

## Priority Justification

Priority 2 (Medium) because:
- Categories are useful but not critical for core functionality
- Workaround exists (edit view)
- Improves UX significantly for organization-heavy users
- Clean implementation pattern exists (Collections)

## Dependencies

None - this is a standalone feature enhancement.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove tag coloring feature
description:
## Goal
Remove the tag coloring functionality from the Book Collection Webapp. This feature allows users to assign colors to tags, but after evaluation it doesn't provide enough value to justify the complexity.

## Scope

### Files to Modify

#### Core Type Definitions
- `src/types/index.ts` - Remove `color` field from `Tag` interface

#### Component Updates
- `src/components/forms/TagInput.tsx`:
  - Remove `TAG_COLORS` constant (lines 9-17)
  - Remove `selectedColor` state variable (line 28)
  - Remove color picker UI section (lines 165-181)
  - Update `handleCreateTag` to not set color (lines 63-85)
  - Simplify `TagBadge` component to remove color dependency (lines 250-283)
  - Remove `getColorClasses` helper (lines 110-113)

- `src/components/forms/TagListing.tsx`:
  - Remove `TAG_COLORS` constant (lines 8-16)
  - Remove `getColorClasses` function (lines 76-79)
  - Update `TagItem` to use neutral styling instead of colored badges
  - Update `TagWithCount` interface (line 21) - remove color field

#### Database Layer
- `src/lib/db.ts` - Consider removing `color` from tags schema index (line 44)

#### Utility Functions
- `src/lib/importUtils.ts`:
  - Remove `generateRandomTagColor` function (lines 176-190)
  - Remove color parameter from `importTagsFromCSV` (line 155)

#### Test Updates
- `src/__tests__/useBookMetadataRefresh.test.ts` - Remove color from mock tag data
- `src/__tests__/metadata-refresh-integration.test.ts` - Remove color from mock tag data

#### Mobile Backend (if applicable)
- `mobile/src/services/api.ts` - Update POST /tags endpoint to not accept color

### Behavior Changes
1. Tags will no longer display colored backgrounds
2. New tags created will not prompt for color selection
3. Existing tag color data should be migrated/cleaned (preserve tag names)
4. Tag input UI will be simplified (no color picker)

## Implementation Order
1. Update types and database schema
2. Update import utilities
3. Update TagInput component
4. Update TagListing component
5. Update all tests
6. Test functionality manually
close_reason: Closed

title: Camera activation turns black instantly - video track recovery loop
description:
# Camera Activation Bug - Video Track Issues

## Summary
Camera activation triggers successfully but immediately turns black. The video track keeps muting/unmuting and entering a failure loop where it cannot maintain a stable video stream.

## Environment
- Using ZXing library for barcode scanning
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts` hook for camera management
- Video resolution: 1280x720

## Error Pattern

### Initial State
```
Video track recovery successful
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video track unmuted
handleTrackUnmute @ useBarcodeScanner.ts:214
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video track muted - attempting recovery
handleTrackMute @ useBarcodeScanner.ts:115
```

### Playback Failure
```
It was not possible to play the video.
BrowserCodeReader2.tryPlayVideo
BrowserCodeReader2.playVideoOnLoad
BrowserCodeReader2.decodeFromVideoElement
attemptRecovery @ useBarcodeScanner.ts:192
handleTrackMute @ useBarcodeScanner.ts:210
Video track muted - attempting recovery
```

### Recovery Loop
```
Video track muted - attempting recovery
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Trying to play video that is already playing.
Video track recovery successful
Video ready: 1280x720, readyState=4, active=true
Video track recovery successful
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video track recovery successful
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video track recovery successful
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video track unmuted
handleTrackUnmute @ useBarcodeScanner.ts:214
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
Video not ready: readyState=0 (need >= 2)
It was not possible to play the video.
```

## Key Issues Identified

1. **readyState=0 Problem**: Video element never reaches readyState >= 2, preventing playback
2. **Mute/Unmute Loop**: Video track keeps getting muted and unmuted continuously
3. **Recovery Failure**: Recovery succeeds temporarily but immediately fails again
4. **"Already Playing" Error**: Attempting to play video that is already playing indicates race condition
5. **No Stable State**: Video briefly shows readyState=4 but quickly reverts to failed state

## Affected Code Locations
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:115` - handleTrackMute
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:189` - Video track recovery successful logging
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:192` - attemptRecovery
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:210` - handleTrackMute
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:214` - handleTrackUnmute
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:233` - Video not ready checking
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:262` - Video ready logging
- `useBarcodeScanner.ts:381` - decodeFromVideoElement call

## Expected Behavior
- Camera should activate and maintain a stable video stream
- Video should remain in readyState >= 2 consistently
- No continuous mute/unmute cycling
- No "Video not ready" errors after initial activation

## Actual Behavior
- Camera activates but displays black screen
- Video stream fails to maintain stable readyState
- Continuous mute/unmute cycling occurs
- Multiple recovery attempts with temporary success followed by failure

## Reproduction Steps
1. Open barcode scanner modal
2. Activate camera
3. Observe black screen instead of camera feed
4. Check console for the error patterns above
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix camera video black screen issue in barcode scanner
description:
## Error Symptoms

```
installHook.js:1 Video track muted
overrideMethod @ installHook.js:1
handleTrackMute @ useBarcodeScanner.ts:116
installHook.js:1 It was not possible to play the video.
```

## Problem Description

Users experience a black camera screen when attempting to use the barcode scanner. The video feed never displays despite the scanner appearing to initialize.

## Root Causes Identified

### 1. Incomplete Track Mute Handler
**Location:** `src/hooks/useBarcodeScanner.ts:115-116`

```typescript
const handleTrackMute = () => {
  console.warn('Video track muted');
};
```

The mute handler only logs a warning but:
- Doesn't update application state to show error to user
- Doesn't attempt stream recovery
- Provides no feedback that something is wrong

### 2. Missing Error State Management
When video track becomes muted:
- `scanState.error` remains `null`
- User sees no error message
- Scanner continues in background with no video
- Poor user experience with no indication of the problem

### 3. No Recovery Strategy
Current implementation has:
- No attempt to restart muted streams
- No fallback to different camera configurations
- No user notification to refresh or retry
- No stream quality degradation or constraint adjustment

### 4. Video Element Configuration
**Location:** `src/components/scanner/BarcodeScannerModal.tsx:143`

```tsx
<video
  ref={videoRef}
  className="absolute inset-0 w-full h-full object-cover"
  playsInline
  muted  // Necessary for autoplay but contributes to mute state confusion
/>
```

The muted attribute is required for autoplay policies but creates confusion when actual track mute events occur.

## Impact

- **User Experience:** Black screen with no feedback, users think app is broken
- **Functionality:** Barcode scanning completely non-functional
- **Reliability:** No automatic recovery from transient issues
- **Debugging:** No visible error state for support

## Technical Details

### Affected Code Locations
1. `src/hooks/useBarcodeScanner.ts:99-133` - Video track monitoring setup
2. `src/hooks/useBarcodeScanner.ts:115-116` - Incomplete handleTrackMute
3. `src/hooks/useBarcodeScanner.ts:294-532` - startScanning with no mute recovery
4. `src/components/scanner/BarcodeScannerModal.tsx:139-144` - Video element

### Browser Considerations
- Autoplay policies require muted video elements
- Track mute events can occur due to:
  - Device power saving
  - Background tab throttling
  - Camera hardware issues
  - Media constraints changes
  - Browser resource management

## Recommended Solutions

### Priority 1: Improve Error Handling (Immediate Fix)
1. Update `handleTrackMute` to set error state:
```typescript
const handleTrackMute = () => {
  console.warn('Video track muted');
  setState(prev => ({ 
    ...prev, 
    error: 'Camera stream was interrupted. Please try again.',
    isScanning: false 
  }));
};
```

2. Add user-facing error message in UI
3. Provide retry mechanism when mute occurs

### Priority 2: Add Recovery Logic
1. Attempt to restart video stream on mute detection
2. Implement graceful degradation (lower resolution)
3. Add automatic retry with backoff strategy

### Priority 3: Enhanced Monitoring
1. Track mute/unmute events in analytics
2. Add detailed logging for support
3. Implement health check for video stream quality

## Testing Requirements

1. Test on iOS Safari (strict autoplay policies)
2. Test on Android Chrome (varying hardware)
3. Test with power saving mode enabled
4. Test with multiple camera apps active
5. Test background/foreground transitions
6. Test with various lighting conditions

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Video displays immediately on scanner open
- [ ] Mute events show clear user-facing error
- [ ] Automatic recovery when possible
- [ ] Manual retry available when automatic recovery fails
- [ ] No black screens during normal operation
- [ ] Tested on iOS and Android browsers
close_reason: Fixed by improving handleTrackMute to set error state and attempt recovery, adding retryScanning function, and adding retry button to UI

title: ISBN barcode scanner shows black screen
description:
## Problem Description

The ISBN barcode scanner is not displaying video correctly. When the scanner is activated:
- Camera indicator light turns on (showing camera is active)
- Only a black screen is displayed instead of the camera feed
- This occurs across multiple browsers
- Camera permissions are granted

## Technical Details

**Current Implementation:**
- Uses `@zxing/library` v0.21.0 for barcode scanning
- Implementation in `src/hooks/useBarcodeScanner.ts` (998 lines)
- UI component in `src/components/scanner/BarcodeScannerModal.tsx`

**Camera Setup Flow:**
1. `startScanning()` function initializes video stream with constraints:
   ```javascript
   const constraints: MediaStreamConstraints = {
     video: {
       facingMode: 'environment',
       width: { ideal: 1280 },
       height: { ideal: 720 }
     }
   };
   ```
2. Uses `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia(constraints)` 
3. Implements extensive timeout handling (10 second timeouts for canplay, loadedmetadata, play)
4. Has retry logic with 3 attempts and 1.5s delays between retries
5. Video element configured with `playsInline muted` attributes

**Potential Issue Areas:**
1. Video element readiness - may not be receiving/displaying frames correctly
2. @zxing/library `decodeFromVideoElement()` may be failing silently
3. Video stream attachment timing issues
4. Browser-specific MediaStream handling differences
5. Video element styling/overlay issues preventing visibility

## Expected Behavior

Camera feed should display in the video element with scanning overlay, allowing users to scan ISBN barcodes.

## Actual Behavior

- Camera light activates (permission working)
- Black screen displayed instead of video feed
- Scanning cannot proceed without visible camera feed

## Testing Performed

- Tested across multiple browsers
- Camera permissions confirmed granted
- Manual ISBN entry still works (confirms app functionality)

## Related Files

- `src/hooks/useBarcodeScanner.ts` - Main scanning logic
- `src/components/scanner/BarcodeScannerModal.tsx` - Scanner UI component

## Suggested Debugging Steps

1. Check if `canplay` and `loadedmetadata` events are firing
2. Verify `video.srcObject` is being set correctly
3. Check browser console for @zxing/library errors
4. Test with different video constraints (remove resolution constraints)
5. Verify video element visibility (z-index, overlay issues)
6. Test on different devices/browsers to identify patterns
close_reason: Resolved: Fixed barcode scanner black screen issue by correcting the video element initialization logic. The original code was waiting for the 'canplay' event before setting videoElement.srcObject, but 'canplay' cannot fire without a source. Fixed by setting srcObject first, then waiting for 'loadedmetadata' event, eliminating the deadlock.

title: Fix react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings
description:
There are 3 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in the codebase that should be fixed:

1. CollectionManager.tsx line 359 - allCollectionsRaw dependency
2. TagInput.tsx line 35 - allTagsRaw dependency  
3. TagInput.tsx line 290 - allTagsRaw dependency

These warnings indicate that useMemo dependencies may change on every render, which can cause performance issues and unexpected behavior. The fix typically involves either:
- Moving the dependency inside the useMemo callback
- Wrapping the initialization in its own useMemo() Hook
- Adding missing dependencies to the dependency array

title: Consolidate 8 critical code duplicates across hooks, components, and utilities
description:
## Code Review Summary

I found **8 critical duplicates** that should be consolidated immediately:

### Priority 1: Duplicate Hooks
| Hook | Locations | Action |
|------|-----------|--------|
| `useOnlineStatus` | `usePerformance.ts:137-157`, `useOffline.ts:63-112` | Keep in `useOffline.ts` (more comprehensive) |
| `useDebounce` | `usePerformance.ts:5-19`, `useOffline.ts:46-60` | Keep in `usePerformance.ts` |
| `useMediaQuery` | `usePerformance.ts:106-122`, `useOffline.ts:21-38` | Keep in `usePerformance.ts` |
| `useIsTouchDevice` | `usePerformance.ts:288-303`, `useOffline.ts:41-43` | Keep in `usePerformance.ts` (more robust) |

### Priority 2: Duplicate Navigation Components
- `SyncStatusIndicator` - Identical in `BottomNavigation.tsx:17-61` and `Navigation.tsx:19-63`
- `MobileMenuOverlay` - Identical in `BottomNavigation.tsx:64-115` and `Navigation.tsx:104-155`
- `BottomNavigation` - Duplicated in both files (Navigation.tsx:158-220 marked "to be removed later")

### Priority 3: Duplicate Utilities
- `getFileExtension` / `getExtensionFromFilename` - functionally identical
- ISBN validation functions in `api.ts:152-193` and `useBarcodeScanner.ts:991-1035`

### Priority 4: Component Conflicts
- `AccessibleModal` (Accessibility.tsx) vs `Modal` (Button.tsx)
- `IconButton` (Accessibility.tsx) vs Button variants

---

## Recommended Consolidation Plan

**Step 1**: Create `src/components/common/SyncStatusIndicator.tsx` and `MobileMenuOverlay.tsx`
**Step 2**: Remove duplicates from `Navigation.tsx`, delete if empty
**Step 3**: Remove duplicate hooks from `useOffline.ts` and `usePerformance.ts`
**Step 4**: Create `src/lib/fileUtils.ts` for file extension functions
**Step 5**: Create `src/lib/isbnUtils.ts` for ISBN utilities
**Step 6**: Decide on single Modal/Button implementation
**Step 7**: Extract `TAG_COLORS` to centralized constants

---

Do you want me to proceed with implementing this consolidation plan? Which priorities should I start with?
close_reason: Consolidated 8 critical code duplicates across hooks, components, and utilities

title: Create Merge Logic and Hooks
Fix TypeScript build errors (50+ errors blocking deployment)
description:
## Issue Summary
The project build fails with 50+ TypeScript errors across multiple files. This needs to be addressed before the project can be considered properly finalized.

## Error Categories Found (50+ errors total)

### 1. JSX Component Issues (src/components/common/Navigation.tsx)
- TS2607: JSX element class does not support attributes 
- TS2786: 'History' cannot be used as a JSX component (type mismatch)

### 2. Component Props Type Errors
- src/components/forms/CollectionManager.tsx:
  - TS2322: Type 'info' not assignable to Badge variant type
  - TS2322: Property 'size' does not exist on BadgeProps
- src/components/forms/StarRating.tsx:
  - TS2722: Cannot invoke possibly undefined object
  - TS2322: Event handler type mismatches (HTMLDivElement vs HTMLButtonElement)

### 3. Missing Type Definitions
- src/hooks/useAnalytics.ts:
  - TS2367: Comparison between ReadingStatus and 'dnf' has no overlap
  - TS2304: Cannot find name 'ReadingLog' (missing import/type)
  - TS2339: Property 'statuses', 'minRating' does not exist on FilterConfig
  - TS18048: 'aDate', 'bDate' possibly undefined
- src/hooks/useSync.ts:
  - TS2339: Property 'getAllByStatus' does not exist on reading log interface
- src/services/syncService.ts:
  - TS2339: Property 'getAllByStatus' does not exist on interface

### 4. Service Worker Issues (src/lib/serviceWorker.ts)
- TS2339: Property 'sync' does not exist on ServiceWorkerRegistration
- TS7006: Parameter 'error' implicitly has 'any' type

### 5. Dynamic Import Type Issues (src/main.tsx)
- TS2322: Promise<typeof import(...)> not assignable to Promise<{ default: ComponentType<any> }>
- TS2307: Cannot find module './pages/History'

### 6. Recharts API Issues (src/pages/Analytics.tsx)
- TS2322: Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'ReactNode'
- TS2769: No overload matches for XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip components
- Property 'data' does not exist on XAxis/YAxis (recharts API change)

### 7. Card Component Issue (src/pages/BookDetail.tsx)
- TS2741: Property 'children' is missing in type '{ className: string; }'

### 8. Book Type Issues (src/pages/Home.tsx)
- TS2339: Property 'readingStatus' does not exist on type 'Book'

## Impact
- Build fails, preventing production deployment
- Multiple missing type definitions suggest incomplete feature implementation
- Some runtime issues likely (e.g., History component, Badge size, readingStatus access)

## Files Requiring Fixes
- src/components/common/Navigation.tsx
- src/components/forms/CollectionManager.tsx
- src/components/forms/StarRating.tsx
- src/hooks/useAnalytics.ts
- src/hooks/useSync.ts
- src/services/syncService.ts
- src/lib/serviceWorker.ts
- src/main.tsx
- src/pages/Analytics.tsx
- src/pages/BookDetail.tsx
- src/pages/Home.tsx

## Priority Assessment
- Priority: P1 (high) - blocks build and deployment
- Type: bug/tech-debt
- Estimated effort: 2-4 hours to fix all issues
close_reason: Resolved: Fixed 50+ TypeScript build errors across 11 files including main.tsx lazy loading types, useAnalytics.ts missing types, CollectionManager.tsx prop issues, useSync.ts and syncService.ts missing methods, Analytics.tsx Recharts API changes, serviceWorker.ts type issues, StarRating.tsx event handlers, BookDetail.tsx Card children, and Home.tsx Book interface.

2. Implement graceful degradation (lower resolution)
3. Add automatic retry with backoff strategy

### Priority 3: Enhanced Monitoring
1. Track mute/unmute events in analytics
2. Add detailed logging for support
3. Implement health check for video stream quality

## Testing Requirements

1. Test on iOS Safari (strict autoplay policies)
2. Test on Android Chrome (varying hardware)
3. Test with power saving mode enabled
4. Test with multiple camera apps active
5. Test background/foreground transitions
6. Test with various lighting conditions

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Video displays immediately on scanner open
- [ ] Mute events show clear user-facing error
- [ ] Automatic recovery when possible
- [ ] Manual retry available when automatic recovery fails
- [ ] No black screens during normal operation
- [ ] Tested on iOS and Android browsers
close_reason: Duplicate of webapp-booky-aqa - same camera video black screen issue

title: Camera Cover Image Capture Support
description:
Implement support for creating cover images via device camera across web and mobile platforms.

**User Story**: As a user, I want to capture cover images using my device's camera so that I can quickly add visual covers to content without needing to upload pre-existing images.

**Technical Requirements**:
- Web: MediaDevices API with getUserMedia()
- iOS: AVFoundation-based custom camera or UIImagePickerController
- Android: CameraX library for lifecycle-aware implementation

**Features**:
- Full-screen camera preview with capture button
- Front/back camera switching
- Automatic image compression and optimization
- Permission handling and graceful fallbacks
- Integration with existing cover image upload flow

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Camera capture accessible from cover image selection UI
- Users can switch between front and rear cameras
- Captured images properly compressed and stored
- Permission denials handled with clear error messages
- All platforms (web, iOS, Android) supported

**Considerations**:
- Memory management for camera preview streams
- Handle app backgrounding on mobile
- User privacy settings respect
close_reason: Closed

title: Create or remove missing History page component
description:
## Issue Summary
The app fails to start because `main.tsx` tries to import a non-existent `./pages/History` module.

## Error Details
```
[vite] Pre-transform error: Failed to resolve import "./pages/History" from "src/main.tsx". Does the file exist?
Internal server error: Failed to resolve import "./pages/History" from "src/main.tsx"
File: src/main.tsx:15:38
```

## Root Cause Analysis
Looking at src/main.tsx, there's a lazy import for HistoryPage:
```tsx
const HistoryPage = lazy(() => import("./pages/History"));
```

But the file `src/pages/History.tsx` does not exist in the pages directory.

## Impact
- App fails to compile/start in development mode
- Production build will also fail
- Navigation to History route will be broken
- This is a P0 blocking issue

## Solution Options

### Option 1: Create the History page (if feature is needed)
- Create `src/pages/History.tsx` with a proper page component
- Implement the History feature as intended
- Estimated effort: 1-2 hours

### Option 2: Remove the History import and route (if not needed)
- Remove the lazy import from main.tsx
- Remove HistoryPage from the routes configuration
- Update routing to not include History
- Estimated effort: 15 minutes

## Decision Required
Before implementing, we need to know:
1. Is the History feature intended to exist? Check project plans/docs
2. If yes, what should the History page contain?
3. If no, remove the import

## Next Steps
- Determine whether to implement or remove the History feature
- Once decided, implement the chosen solution
close_reason: Resolved: Removed unused History import from BottomNavigation.tsx and cleaned up orphaned History navigation item. The History page import issue in main.tsx was already fixed previously.

title: Fix ESLint configuration for ES modules
description:
## Issue Summary
The ESLint configuration is broken due to using `__dirname` in an ES module scope.

## Error Details
```
ESLint: 8.57.1
ReferenceError: __dirname is not defined in ES module scope
This file is being treated as an ES module because it has a '.js' file extension and 'package.json' contains "type": "module". To treat it as a CommonJS script, rename it to use the '.cjs' file extension.
    at eslint.config.js:13:26
```

## Root Cause
The file eslint.config.js uses `__dirname` which is not available in ES modules, but the project has `"type": "module"` in package.json.

## Solution Options
1. Rename eslint.config.js to eslint.config.cjs (CommonJS)
2. Update eslint.config.js to use ES module compatible code:
   - Use `import.meta.url` or `fileURLToPath` instead of `__dirname`
   - Or use path.dirname() with import.meta.url

## Impact
- Cannot run linter to check code quality
- Prevents automated code quality checks in CI/CD
- Need to fix before proper finalization
close_reason: Resolved: Fixed ESLint ES module configuration by replacing __dirname with ES module compatible code using fileURLToPath and createRequire, added TypeScript parser, and installed missing eslint-plugin-react dependency.

title: Implement metadata refresh button for books
description:
Add a refresh button to book detail and edit pages that allows users to re-fetch metadata from external APIs (Open Library, Google Books). This enables updating book information if external sources have been updated or if initial import was incomplete.

## Requirements
- Add refresh button to BookDetailPage header
- Add refresh button to EditBookPage
- Use existing useBookLookup hook to re-fetch metadata
- Show loading state during refresh
- Preserve existing user data (ratings, tags, collections) when refreshing
- Handle API errors gracefully
- Show success/error feedback via toast notifications

## Technical Details
- Reuse existing searchByISBN and searchGoogleBooksByISBN from api.ts
- Update book in IndexedDB while preserving local-only fields
- Consider: What fields to update vs preserve?
  - Update: title, authors, description, coverUrl, publisher, publishedYear, pageCount, categories, subjects, ratings
  - Preserve: localOnly fields, reading status, tags, collections, user ratings/reviews
close_reason:
Successfully implemented metadata refresh button for books with the following features:
- Created useBookMetadataRefresh hook with priority-based API lookup (Google Books > Open Library)
- Added refresh button to BookDetailPage header with loading state and toast notifications
- Added refresh button to EditBookPage header with loading state and toast notifications
- Implemented proper error handling and user feedback
- Preserves user data (ratings, tags, collections) during metadata refresh
- All tests pass and build succeeds

title: Backend Implementation - Epic
description:
Implement complete backend infrastructure for persistent data storage, user authentication, and cross-device synchronization. This epic transforms the app from a local-only storage system to a full-stack application with cloud sync capabilities.

**Current State:**
- Frontend: Fully functional offline-first PWA with IndexedDB
- Backend: Minimal API proxy with no persistence
- Sync: Client-side only with TODO markers

**Target State:**
- Full backend with PostgreSQL database
- User authentication system
- RESTful API for all CRUD operations
- Real-time data synchronization
- Persistent file storage for images

**Key Benefits:**
- User data persists across devices
- Cloud backup prevents data loss
- Cross-device sync capabilities
- User accounts and profiles
- Collaborative features (future)

**Related Tickets:**
- Database Architecture & Schema Design (depends on)
- User Authentication System (depends on)
- User Data Sync API (depends on)
- File Storage for Book Covers (depends on)
- API Rate Limiting & Security (depends on)
- Backend Integration Tests (depends on)
- Client-Side Sync Integration (depends on)
- Backend Deployment & CI/CD (depends on)
close_reason: Backend Implementation Epic complete! All 8 tasks finished: 1) Database Architecture, 2) Authentication System, 3) Sync API, 4) File Storage, 5) Rate Limiting & Security, 6) Integration Tests, 7) Client-Side Sync, 8) Deployment & CI/CD. Backend is production-ready with PostgreSQL, JWT auth, RESTful APIs, Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboards, Nginx reverse proxy, structured logging, and complete operations runbook.
